category: Frameworks, mobile, games, hardware, and creative ecosystems

surface.name: Create your first Android app
surface.selection_basis: Android's beginner Compose course presents a codelab explicitly named Create your first Android app. It defines the app to build, the prerequisites, the exact Empty Activity project configuration, all Compose edits, and a final personalized preview before its conclusion.

alternatives_considered.name: Your first program in Kotlin
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is an earlier programming-foundations codelab in Kotlin Playground and explicitly cannot build Android apps.

alternatives_considered.name: Build a simple app with text composables
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This later codelab assumes the developer already knows how to create and run an Android Studio app.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create the first Android app with Android Studio, Kotlin, and Jetpack Compose and verify its personalized greeting in Preview.
assumed_prior_state: Discovery is complete and the developer has selected native Android development with Kotlin and Jetpack Compose.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: You built your first Android app in Compose
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: Android Studio has built a Greeting Card Compose app whose Preview shows a personalized introduction with a selected background color and 24.dp padding.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: After adding padding and its imports, the Compose Preview renders the personalized Hi, my name is ... text on the selected colored Surface with spacing around the text, followed by the codelab's congratulations statement.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: After the padding section, the codelab states: congratulations, you built your first Android app in Compose, then moves to solution review and conclusion.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: It is the first-app codelab's explicit completion statement after all required creation and customization actions. Running on an emulator or physical device is explicitly deferred to later codelabs.

prerequisites.requirement: The latest Android Studio installed with its required SDK components.
A supported 64-bit Windows, macOS, Linux, or ChromeOS computer with internet access and resources meeting Android Studio's requirements.

primary_path.action: If needed, complete the official Android Studio installation codelab for the computer's operating system.
details: Verify system requirements, download Android Studio from the official page, read and accept the license if agreed, install with the documented defaults, allow required operating-system prompts, wait for SDK components, and finish at the Welcome window.
primary_path.input: Supported computer, internet access, and installation permission
primary_path.output: Latest Android Studio and SDK components
primary_path.success_signal: The Welcome to Android Studio window states the environment is ready to create apps.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Downloads and component installation can take time; platform-specific security, administrator, firewall, or license prompts can block setup.

primary_path.action: Launch Android Studio and click New Project in the Welcome dialog.
primary_path.success_signal: The template list appears.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No wait is documented.

primary_path.action: Select the Phone and Tablet tab, choose Empty Activity, and click Next.
details: Empty Activity supplies a single-screen Compose app whose starter UI displays Hello Android!
primary_path.success_signal: Project fields are visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Choosing another template changes the generated code and documented preview.

primary_path.action: Set Name to Greeting Card.
primary_path.success_signal: Greeting Card appears in the Name field.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No wait is documented.

primary_path.action: Leave Package name as com.example.greetingcard and leave Save location at its default, noting the location.
primary_path.success_signal: The documented package name and a known save location are set.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Changing the package makes later imports and solution code differ.

primary_path.action: Select API 24: Android 7.0 (Nougat) as Minimum SDK and click Finish.
primary_path.success_signal: Android Studio starts generating and syncing the project.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A different minimum SDK changes device compatibility but not the Preview-based endpoint.

primary_path.action: Wait for project setup and Gradle sync to finish.
details: The codelab explicitly says this may take a while and shows progress and completion messages.
primary_path.success_signal: The setup progress finishes and project files are available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Network, Gradle, or SDK-component failures can block sync.

primary_path.action: Close the What's New pane if it appears, then click Split in the upper-right editor controls.
details: Split shows Project, Code, and Design areas together.
primary_path.success_signal: Project, code, and preview areas are visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The What's New pane is conditional and can be skipped when absent.

primary_path.action: If the Design pane is blank, click Build & Refresh and wait for the Compose Preview build.
primary_path.success_signal: The preview displays Hello Android!
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The build can take a while; build or sync errors prevent Preview rendering.

primary_path.action: Inspect the Project tab in Android view, switch to Project Source Files, then switch back to Android view.
details: The codelab uses this sequence to orient the developer to the generated package and the two file hierarchies.
primary_path.output: Developer located in the expected Android project view
primary_path.success_signal: The com.example.greetingcard package is visible and Android view is restored.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If the hierarchy looks different later, the guide says to confirm Android view is selected.

primary_path.action: In MainActivity.kt, change Greeting's Text value from Hello $name! to Hi, my name is $name!.
primary_path.success_signal: Preview automatically shows the new introduction text.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A syntax error prevents Preview compilation.

primary_path.action: In GreetingPreview, replace Android with the developer's name, rebuild if needed, and inspect the personalized preview.
details: The guide's example name is Meghan.
primary_path.success_signal: The preview introduces the chosen name.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If Preview does not update automatically, rebuild it.

primary_path.action: Highlight the Text line, press Alt+Enter on Windows or Option+Enter on macOS, and select Surround with widget.
primary_path.success_signal: The container choices appear.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The keyboard shortcut differs by operating system.

primary_path.action: Choose Surround with Container, then replace the generated Box with Surface().
primary_path.success_signal: The Greeting function contains Surface around Text.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Leaving Box in place does not match the following color instructions.

primary_path.action: Add color = Color to the Surface parameters.
details: Color initially appears unresolved, which is the expected intermediate state.
primary_path.success_signal: Color is shown as an unresolved reference ready for import.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No wait is documented.

primary_path.action: Expand the imports and add import androidx.compose.ui.graphics.Color.
primary_path.success_signal: Color resolves but remains incomplete until a specific color is selected.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A wrong import leaves the reference unresolved.

primary_path.action: Use Help search or Code > Optimize Imports to alphabetize imports and remove unused imports.
primary_path.success_signal: Imports match the codelab's organized list.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No wait is documented.

primary_path.action: Add a period after Color and choose a color suggestion, using Color.Cyan for the documented route.
details: The codelab permits another favorite color.
primary_path.success_signal: The Preview updates with the selected colored background.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An invalid constant prevents compilation.

primary_path.action: Add .padding(24.dp) to the Text modifier.
primary_path.success_signal: padding and dp initially request their imports if absent.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Unresolved padding or dp references require the documented imports.

primary_path.action: Add imports for androidx.compose.foundation.layout.padding and androidx.compose.ui.unit.dp, then optimize imports.
primary_path.success_signal: No unresolved import remains and Preview can rebuild.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Import or syntax errors block the final Preview.

primary_path.action: Inspect the final Compose Preview.
details: The complete code wraps personalized Text in a colored Surface and applies 24.dp padding.
primary_path.output: First Android app rendered in Preview
primary_path.success_signal: The personalized greeting, chosen background color, and padding all render, matching the codelab's explicit first-app completion statement.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Use Build & Refresh and resolve any compiler errors if Preview is stale or blank.

branches.condition: Android Studio is already installed and current with required SDK components.
branches.path: Skip the installation codelab and begin at the Welcome to Android Studio window.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Removes system checks, license acceptance, downloads, security prompts, and setup-wizard waits.

branches.condition: The computer runs macOS or Linux instead of Windows.
branches.path: Follow the matching official installation section, including chip-appropriate download on macOS or archive extraction, studio.sh, SDK and NDK licenses, and setup-wizard prompts on Linux.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes installation actions but rejoins at the same Android Studio Welcome window.

branches.condition: The What's New pane does not appear.
branches.path: Skip closing it and select Split directly.

branches.condition: Preview does not update automatically after code changes.
branches.path: Use Build & Refresh and wait for Preview compilation.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds a manual rebuild and wait before visual verification.

branches.path: Use Option+Enter instead of Alt+Enter to open the surround action.

branches.condition: The developer prefers another supported color.
branches.path: Choose another Color constant instead of Cyan and continue with padding.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The final background differs while still meeting the guide's customizable outcome.

friction_gates.description: Android Studio, SDK components, system requirements, license acceptance, large downloads, and operating-system security prompts precede project creation when the IDE is absent.
Initial project setup and Gradle sync may take a while before the source and Preview are ready.
The first Compose Preview build can take a while and requires Build & Refresh when the pane is blank.
The developer must perform multiple Kotlin and Compose edits, imports, refactoring actions, and Preview rebuilds before explicit completion.

sources.title: Create your first Android app
sections_used: Create a project using the template

sources.title: Download and install Android Studio
sections_used: Windows: Download and install Android Studio
macOS: Download and install Android Studio
Linux: Download and install Android Studio

uncertainties.question: Does the current latest Android Studio present exactly the same template and setup labels shown in the codelab?
uncertainties.impact: Minor UI labels or screenshots may differ during project creation.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The codelab itself warns that Android Studio is updated and the current UI may look different, while preserving the same intended actions.

excluded_after_success: Solution-code comparison after the completed app
Running the app on an emulator
Running on a physical Android device
